perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of Netherlands, excluding its rules for choice of law. Notwithstanding an action for injunction or other equitable remedy, all disputes relating to or arising out of or in connection with thisAgreement shall be finally settled by arbitration in accordance with the Rules of the ArbitrationInstitute of the Amsterdam Chamber of Commerce. The arbitration shall be held in theNetherlands, Amsterdam and the English language shall be used in the proceedings. Thearbitration decision shall be final and binding on the parties and enforceable in any court ofcompetent jurisdiction.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of Netherlands, excluding its rules for choice of law. Notwithstanding an action for injunction or other equitable remedy. The arbitration decision shall be final and binding on the parties and enforceable in any court ofcompetent jurisdiction. All disputes relating to or arising out of or in connection with thisAgreement shall be finally settled by arbitration in accordance with the Rules of the ArbitrationInstitute of the Amsterdam Chamber of Commerce. The arbitration shall be held in theNetherlands, Amsterdam and the English language shall be used in the proceedings.
perturbation.explanation: The removal of the clause 'This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of Netherlands' and placing it within another sentence weakens the enforceability of the governing law provision. This can create ambiguity regarding which jurisdiction's laws apply, potentially leading to disputes over contract interpretation and enforcement.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Burgerlijk Wetboek Boek 6 Artikel 247 (Netherlands Civil Code Book 6 Article 247)
perturbation.law_explanation: This law ensures that specific articles (231-244) of Book 6 of the Civil Code, which protect parties from unfair terms, cannot be circumvented in contracts with professionals. By obscuring the governing law, the contract makes it harder to invoke these protections, since its unclear if the Netherlands laws still applies.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement may be modified only in writing signed byboth parties hereto.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall apply to any CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION that may have beenprovided to the RECEIVING PARTY prior to or after the date hereof, and shall continue to govern thedelivery of CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION until terminated by written notice from either party to theother, except that the obligations of the parties hereunder with regard to CONFIDENTIALINFORMATION disclosed prior to termination shall continue for a period for two (2) years thereafter. This Agreement may be modified only in writing signed byboth parties hereto.
perturbation.explanation: Hiding the modification clause inside the duration section implies it only applies to changes in the duration of the contract and not all other aspects. This could be a problem as any modification of the contract, according to Article 6:217 BW, needs to be agreed upon by both parties and clearly communicated. By placing it under 'duration' it creates ambiguity that any other modification is allowed without both parties consent.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Burgerlijk Wetboek Boek 6 Artikel 217 (Netherlands Civil Code Book 6 Article 217)
perturbation.law_explanation: Article 6:217 states that a contract is formed by an offer and acceptance. If the modification can be read to only apply to 'duration', it contradicts the requirement that any agreement modifications need mutual consent.

perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly states that any modification to the agreement requires a written document signed by both parties. The changed text buries this clause within a section regarding the duration of the agreement, potentially implying that the modification clause only applies to changes in the duration, not to other aspects of the contract. This creates a structural flaw, as defined in the prompt, where a legally required disclosure (agreement modification) is placed in a potentially misleading section. The Law Explanation states that Article 6:217 requires mutual consent for modifications, and the perturbation explanation suggests that the new placement could be interpreted as allowing modifications without mutual consent. While the scraped snippets are not directly helpful in supporting this, the combination of the original/changed text, perturbation explanation, and law explanation supports the assessment that there is a structural flaw that creates a legal contradiction because it undermines the requirement for mutual consent in contract modifications. This is not a minor weakening or ambiguity but a potentially serious flaw that could lead to disputes about the validity of modifications to the agreement.

perturbation.original_text: In case of unauthorized use or disclosure of the CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION,The DISCLOSING PARTY shall be entitled to liquidated damages in the amount of €10 000 (tenthousand Euro) for each such use or disclosure. Notwithstanding the right to liquidated damages,the DISCLOSING PARTY has the right to take any measures available and to claim and receivea higher amount of compensation if the DISCLOSING PARTY can prove that the actual damagesustained will exceed the amount of liquidated damages.
perturbation.changed_text: In case of unauthorized use or disclosure of the CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION,The DISCLOSING PARTY shall be entitled to liquidated damages in the amount of €10 000 (tenthousand Euro) for each such use or disclosure. Notwithstanding the right to liquidated damages, the DISCLOSING PARTY has the right to take any measures available and to claim and receivea higher amount of compensation.
perturbation.explanation: Removing the last part of the sentence that clarifies the DISCLOSING party can claim higher amounts, limits the amount the disclosing party can ask, even if the damages exceeded the €10000 agreed previously. This goes against the compensatory damages provided by law.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Burgerlijk Wetboek Boek 6 Artikel 96 (Netherlands Civil Code Book 6 Article 96)
perturbation.law_explanation: This article states that damages must compensate for losses incurred, not limit them without justification. Removing the phrase goes against compensatory damage principles, because it limits the amount, even though there are higher damages.

perturbation.justification: The original contract text allows the DISCLOSING PARTY to claim a higher amount of compensation if the actual damages exceed the liquidated damages. The changed text removes the phrase 'if the DISCLOSING PARTY can prove that the actual damages sustained will exceed the amount of liquidated damages,' limiting the DISCLOSING PARTY to only claiming a higher amount of compensation, regardless of whether the actual damages exceed the initial amount. The Law Explanation supports this by stating that the changed text limits the amount, even though there are higher damages, thus contradicting compensatory damage principles. While there are no relevant scraped snippets, the Perturbation and Law Explanations, along with the Original and Changed text comparison, show that the change directly restricts the DISCLOSING PARTY's ability to claim full compensation for actual damages incurred, leading to a legal contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The RECEIVING PARTY understands and agrees that monetary damages will not besufficient to avoid or compensate for the unauthorized use or disclosure of the CONFIDENTIALINFORMATION and that injunctive relief would be appropriate to prevent any actual or threateneduse of disclosure of such CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION.
perturbation.changed_text: The RECEIVING PARTY understands and agrees that monetary damages will not besufficient to avoid or compensate for the unauthorized use or disclosure of the CONFIDENTIALINFORMATION.
perturbation.explanation: Removing that injunctive relief would be appropriate to prevent actual or threatened use of disclosure of such confidential information makes that the first resort of the AG Project would be financial compensation instead of seeking an injunction in case of confidential information being revealed. The removal of the clause limits the disclosing parties options and gives more freedom for the receiving party. This contradicts Dutch law on remedies for breach of contract.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Burgerlijk Wetboek Boek 3 Artikel 296 (Netherlands Civil Code Book 3 Article 296)
perturbation.law_explanation: This article establishes the right to specific performance, including injunctive relief, unless otherwise specified. Removing the acknowledgement that injunctive relief is appropriate might be interpreted as a waiver of that right or limit its availability, thus contradicting the full range of remedies afforded under Dutch law. There is no explicit intention anymore to seek injunctive relief and the intention would be for monetary compensation.
